rag_rules: AGREE annex blocks must be placed in component type scopes for assumptions/guarantees and implementation scopes for bindings.
Implementation-level contracts should only reference visible outputs and declared auxiliary variables.
generic_rules: Use component type annexes for contracts that reference component ports and inputs.
Use implementation annexes for contracts that bind to subcomponent features or connections.
Do not reference subcomponent ports in component type contracts unless represented through variables.
Preserve AADL architecture by placing AGREE annexes in valid scopes relative to component structure.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
Assignments in implementation scope must reference existing output features or declared variables.
anti_patterns: Avoid placing implementation-level bindings in component type scopes.
Do not reference subcomponent ports in component type annexes unless properly represented.
irrelevant_topics: AADL Property Names Are Not AGREE Variables chunk 1
